ops.title: Fleet AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md/install-binary.sh/settings.json are cc-guides-rendered — edit fragments + render + commit fragments/artifacts/lock together; provenance in cc-guides.lock, artifacts carry version-free markers (json none)

ops.body:
As of 2026-07-10 (v3.1 lock model), fleet repos render `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, install-binary.sh where adopted, and (migrated repos) `.claude/settings.json` from `.claude/fragments/<target-path>/` layout dirs. md/sh artifacts carry only a version-free `<!-- GENERATED … -->` marker and json artifacts nothing; ALL provenance (binary version, full-sha pins, managed-target registry) lives in `.claude/fragments/cc-guides.lock`.

**Why:** an edit to the rendered artifact alone passes locally and pushes fine, then the next daily re-render composes from the unchanged fragments and lands a `chore: re-render guides` commit that silently reverts it (observed live in the v1 era: a 31-repo re-stamp "succeeded" 29/31, only 14 survived). The `Guides` check re-composes from the lock's pins and reds on drift.

**How to apply:** edit the local `*.fragment.*` files (never the artifact), `cc-guides render`, commit fragments + artifacts + lock in ONE commit; verify against main@origin after Guides CI settles. Shared prose changes go in cc-skills `plugin/guides/` — each repo's daily cron picks them up, or force with the `gh api … cc-guides-render` dispatch loop. Settings deltas go in `settings-overrides.fragment.json` (union-merge cannot remove a base entry — removals require moving the key out of the shared fragment). cc-context plugin ≥0.9.0 enforces: PreToolUse blocks direct edits of managed artifacts. See [[cc-guides-v3-architecture]]. Related: [[model-routing-convention]], [[shared-working-copy-concurrency]].
